Settlement.text: {=!}Gangapur lies on the Godavari where the river emerges from the rocky plateau country, a crossing village of importance where the road from Aurangabad to Osmanabad fords the water, its ghat perpetually busy with pilgrims heading for the temples upstream.

Settlement.name: {=!}Gulbarga
Settlement.text: {=!}Gulbarga was the first capital of the Bahmani Sultanate and its mosques and fortifications still speak of a time when this Deccan city rivaled Delhi in power and Islamic learning.

Settlement.name: {=!}Chincholi
Settlement.text: {=!}Chincholi controls the road between Gulbarga and the Krishna River crossings, its market fueled by the cattle and cotton trade of the surrounding black-soil plateau.

Settlement.name: {=!}Shahabad
Settlement.text: {=!}Shahabad is a limestone and lime-producing village whose kilns supply the construction trade of the surrounding Deccan towns, their fires visible for miles across the plateau at night.

Settlement.name: {=!}Golconda
Settlement.text: {=!}Golconda village is the bazaar that grew outside the famous diamond fortress, where gem traders and jewelers have conducted their business under the shadow of the citadel for generations.

Settlement.name: {=!}Qutbullapur
Settlement.text: {=!}Qutbullapur is a northern suburb of Hyderabad supplying the great city with market gardens and dairy, its gardens watered by channels dug under the Qutb Shahi sultans.

Settlement.name: {=!}Khargaon
Settlement.text: {=!}Khargaon is a river-crossing village on the upper Narmada where the ancient ford has made it a market hub for goods moving between the Deccan plateau and the cotton-growing country of central India.

Settlement.name: {=!}Sendhwa
Settlement.text: {=!}Sendhwa is a river port on the Tapti where country boats load cotton and sesame oil destined for the downstream markets of Surat and the busy harbor of the Gulf of Khambhat.

Settlement.name: {=!}Nimad
Settlement.text: {=!}Nimad is the fertile river-bottom country of the Narmada valley below Burhanpur, a string of villages growing cotton, millet, and lentils in the black soil that local farmers say can swallow a plough whole in the monsoon season.

Settlement.name: {=!}Bhadravati
Settlement.text: {=!}Bhadravati is an iron-smelting village on the Wardha River where local blacksmiths work iron ore from the surrounding Vidarbha hills into tools for the agricultural villages of central Deccan.

Settlement.name: {=!}Yeotmal
Settlement.text: {=!}Yeotmal is a cotton-market village in the heart of Vidarbha's cotton belt, its seasonal markets drawing traders from Nagpur to Amravati for the white gold of the Deccan plateau.

Settlement.name: {=!}Ibrahimpatnam
Settlement.text: {=!}Ibrahimpatnam is a small settlement below Golconda's walls on the road south toward the Krishna River, its inhabitants employed in the lime-burning kilns that once supplied mortar for the great fortress and now serve the masonry needs of the growing Hyderabad city downstream.

Settlement.name: {=!}Kothur
Settlement.text: {=!}Kothur is a diamond-washing village in the Krishna valley below Golconda where the famous gem deposits were worked by thousands of laborers in the Qutb Shahi era. The great mines are exhausted, but washers still sift the river gravel seeking the rare uncut stones that occasionally surface.

Settlement.name: {=!}Rohtas
Settlement.text: {=!}Rohtas is the hill district that takes its name from the Bihar fort, its villages supplying labour and provisions to the garrison perched on its mesa above the Son River.

Settlement.name: {=!}Sasaram
Settlement.text: {=!}Sasaram is Sher Shah Suri's tomb-town, the magnificent octagonal mausoleum of the great Afghan administrator rising from a lake in the center of the town he was born in.

Settlement.name: {=!}Pandua
Settlement.text: {=!}Pandua was the first capital of the Bengal Sultanate before Gaur, its mosques and palace ruins scattered across farmland that the jungle is slowly reclaiming.

Settlement.name: {=!}Malda
Settlement.text: {=!}Malda is a silk-trading district town on the Mahananda River where the famous Malda silk and muslin are produced for the export trade through Bengal's river ports.

Settlement.name: {=!}Pakur
Settlement.text: {=!}Pakur is a stone-quarrying village in the Rajmahal hills whose granite supplies the construction of the riverside fort and the temples of the Ganges plain.

Settlement.name: {=!}Sahebganj
Settlement.text: {=!}Sahebganj is a river port on the Ganges opposite the Rajmahal hills where goods from the Bihar interior are loaded onto country boats for the downstream journey to Calcutta.

Settlement.name: {=!}Kalna
Settlement.text: {=!}Kalna is a temple town on the Bhagirathi River in Burdwan district famous for its cluster of terracotta temples, the Bengali artisans' craft at its most exuberant.

Settlement.name: {=!}Katwa
Settlement.text: {=!}Katwa is a river-junction market on the Bhagirathi where the Ajay River meets the main stream, its ghats busy with boats carrying grain and jute from the Bengal interior.

Settlement.name: {=!}Habiganj
Settlement.text: {=!}Habiganj is a market town in eastern Bengal where the tea trade from the Sylhet hills meets the river merchants of the Meghna system.

Settlement.name: {=!}Sunamganj
Settlement.text: {=!}Sunamganj is a haor village in the wetlands of Sylhet district where the seasonal flooding creates a vast inland sea and the fishing communities move with the waters.

Settlement.name: {=!}Jhargram
Settlement.text: {=!}Jhargram is a forest-edge town in the Midnapore laterite country where the Jungle Mahal begins and the Bengali plains fade into the forested hills of the Adivasi heartland.

Settlement.name: {=!}Ghatal
Settlement.text: {=!}Ghatal is a river town on the Silai River in Midnapore district where the jute and rice of the Bengal plains are loaded for the downstream journey to Calcutta.

Settlement.name: {=!}Arrah
Settlement.text: {=!}Arrah is a district market town in Bihar on the Son River plain where the saltpeter, grain, and opium trades of the interior are assembled for the Patna merchants.

Settlement.name: {=!}Buxar
Settlement.text: {=!}Buxar is famous as the battlefield where the Mughals under Humayun fought and lost to Sher Shah Suri, a name that marks the end of one dynasty and the beginning of another.

Settlement.name: {=!}Katihar
Settlement.text: {=!}Katihar is a river junction town in northeast Bihar where the Ganga and Mahananda meet, its position making it a natural marketplace for the trade between Bihar and eastern Bengal.

Settlement.name: {=!}Araria
Settlement.text: {=!}Araria is a market village in the north Bihar tarai where the routes from Nepal enter the Gangetic plain, busy with traders carrying hill goods to the Bihar markets.

Settlement.name: {=!}Paghman
Settlement.text: {=!}Paghman is a cool mountain village above Kabul renowned for its orchards and shaded gardens where the Afghan nobility retreat from the summer heat of the city below.

Settlement.name: {=!}Istalif
Settlement.text: {=!}Istalif is famous for its distinctive blue-green glazed pottery, the village's craftsmen maintaining a tradition of ceramic art that has continued unbroken since the Timurid era.

Settlement.name: {=!}Muqur
Settlement.text: {=!}Muqur is a waystation on the Ghazni-Kandahar road where caravans water their camels and exchange news from the north and south, the crossroads of Afghanistan's central plains.

Settlement.name: {=!}Giro
Settlement.text: {=!}Giro is a small farming community in the Ghazni district living from its winter wheat and the dried apricots that sustain the mountain communities through the long Afghan cold season.

Settlement.name: {=!}Charsadda
Settlement.text: {=!}Charsadda occupies the site of ancient Pushkalavati, the Gandharan capital, its fields among the most fertile in the borderlands between the Hindu Kush and the Indus.

Settlement.name: {=!}Nowshera
Settlement.text: {=!}Nowshera guards the river crossing at the junction of the Kabul and Landai rivers where armies have forded since ancient times and the shoals shift every monsoon to create new channels.

Settlement.name: {=!}Kama
Settlement.text: {=!}Kama is an orchard village east of Jalalabad on the Kabul River known for its pomegranates and mulberries, the summer harvest sold to the markets of Kabul and Peshawar.

Settlement.name: {=!}Khugiani
Settlement.text: {=!}Khugiani is a Pashtun tribal village where every man carries a musket, hospitality is extended generously to guests, and the blood feud is maintained with equal fidelity.

Settlement.name: {=!}Surkhrud
Settlement.text: {=!}Surkhrud is a red-earthed village on the Kabul-Khyber road where the spring rains briefly transform the plain into meadows of wildflowers before the summer bakes the land to dust.

Settlement.name: {=!}Mazar
Settlement.text: {=!}Mazar is a shrine village in the Kunduz foothills where a local saint's tomb draws the faithful of the surrounding tribes for the annual urs celebration.

Settlement.name: {=!}Khulm
Settlement.text: {=!}Khulm is a prosperous caravan city in the upper Amu Darya reaches where merchants from Balkh, Kabul, and Bukhara trade horses, silk, and the metalwork of the Central Asian workshops.

Settlement.name: {=!}Guzara
Settlement.text: {=!}Guzara is a garden village near Herat famous for its grapes and melons, the Herati fruit considered the finest in Central Asia and sold as far as the markets of Persia and India.

Settlement.name: {=!}Shindand
Settlement.text: {=!}Shindand is a waystation on the long road between Herat and Kandahar offering shelter from the wind-scoured plain to caravans crossing the empty heart of Afghanistan.

Settlement.name: {=!}Karukh
Settlement.text: {=!}Karukh is a Tajik-speaking village in the Herat district renowned for its carpet weavers who produce the deep-red Herati patterns sold in bazaars from Kabul to Isfahan.

Settlement.name: {=!}Arghandab
Settlement.text: {=!}Arghandab is a green oasis on the river of the same name where pomegranate orchards and vineyards make it the most prosperous agricultural settlement in the Kandahar environs.

Settlement.name: {=!}Daman
Settlement.text: {=!}Daman is a flat-land farming village in the Kandahar district that relies on the winter rains of the Afghan south rather than irrigation, its wheat harvests feeding the city's garrison.

Settlement.name: {=!}Logar
Settlement.text: {=!}Logar is a river valley province south of Kabul whose farming villages supply the Afghan capital with grain, fruit, and the coal mined from the surrounding mountains.

Settlement.name: {=!}Maidan
Settlement.text: {=!}Maidan is an open plain southwest of Kabul where the Hazara communities farm wheat and tend orchards in the shadow of the mountain ranges.

Settlement.name: {=!}Landi
Settlement.text: {=!}Landi is a Khyber tribal village at the mouth of the pass where the Afridi traders who have controlled the route since antiquity maintain their customary toll-collection.

Settlement.name: {=!}Jamrud
Settlement.text: {=!}Jamrud is the Sikh-built fort village at the eastern Khyber entrance, the last post of settled government before the tribal territory of the pass begins.

Settlement.name: {=!}Yakawlang
Settlement.text: {=!}Yakawlang is a high-altitude Hazara village in the central Hindu Kush where the short summer growing season produces little more than subsistence grain and the winter lasts eight months.

Settlement.name: {=!}Panjao
Settlement.text: {=!}Panjao is a Hazara farming village in the Bamyan district eking a living from the harsh mountain soil while the ruins of the Buddhas tower above the valley floor.

Settlement.name: {=!}Bakwa
Settlement.text: {=!}Bakwa is a desert-edge village in the Farah district where the seasonal floods from the Afghan mountains are harvested for a brief agriculture before the land bakes dry again.

Settlement.name: {=!}Gulistan
Settlement.text: {=!}Gulistan is a small oasis village in the Farah region whose name means Garden but whose reality is a scrubby settlement barely sustaining life in the semi-arid Sistan borderland.

Settlement.name: {=!}Girishk
Settlement.text: {=!}Girishk is a fortified village on the Helmand River controlling a key crossing between the Kandahar province and the routes to Persia.

Settlement.name: {=!}Nadali
Settlement.text: {=!}Nadali is a river-bank village in the Helmand valley where the irrigation channels of the Ghaznavid era, partly restored, sustain a modest agriculture in the semi-desert.

Settlement.name: {=!}Baghlan
Settlement.text: {=!}Baghlan is a fertile river valley district north of the Hindu Kush where cotton, sugarcane, and fruit trees benefit from the longer growing season at lower altitude.

Settlement.name: {=!}Khumri
Settlement.text: {=!}Khumri is a mining and market village in the Baghlan district where coal deposits have been worked since ancient times to fuel the hearths of the northern Afghan provinces.

Settlement.name: {=!}Tulak
Settlement.text: {=!}Tulak is a remote village in the Ghor mountains accessible only through passes that close in winter, its population of Tajik farmers isolated from the wider world for months at a time.

Settlement.name: {=!}Shahrak
Settlement.text: {=!}Shahrak is a highland village in the Ghor district where the summers are cool enough for wheat and potatoes, the villagers spending the winter months weaving the heavy carpets that sustain their income.

Settlement.name: {=!}Nimroz
Settlement.text: {=!}Nimroz is the desert province of the old Sistan basin where the Helmand River disappears into marshes and the seasonal floods determine the rhythm of an ancient agricultural civilization.

Settlement.name: {=!}Dilaram
Settlement.text: {=!}Dilaram is a waystation on the Herat-Kandahar road where caravans rest before crossing the long waterless stretch of the Afghan southern desert.

Settlement.name: {=!}Mandya
Settlement.text: {=!}Mandya is the sugarcane capital of the Cauvery delta where the mill chimneys pour smoke during the crushing season and the jaggery is stacked in golden blocks for the Mysore market.

Settlement.name: {=!}Malavalli
Settlement.text: {=!}Malavalli is a silk-cocoon village in the Cauvery irrigation belt where mulberry cultivation has created a cottage industry supplying the weavers of the Mysore silk trade.

Settlement.name: {=!}Maddur
Settlement.text: {=!}Maddur is a crossroads market village on the Bangalore-Mysore road famous for the fried lentil vada sold at its wayside stalls, a refreshment for travellers that has made the village's name throughout southern Karnataka.

Settlement.name: {=!}Holalkere
Settlement.text: {=!}Holalkere is a limestone plateau village in Chitradurga district where the rocky landscape preserves the ruins of Vijayanagara-era fortifications and the Kuruba shepherds graze their flocks on the thin highland grass.

Settlement.name: {=!}Hiriyur
Settlement.text: {=!}Hiriyur is a market town in Chitradurga where cotton, groundnut, and jowar from the Deccan farms are exchanged in the weekly market that draws traders from a wide radius of the Karnataka plateau.

Settlement.name: {=!}Shimoga
Settlement.text: {=!}Shimoga is a teak-trading town on the Tunga River where the forest timber of the Western Ghats is assembled and sold to the coastal merchants who supply Malabar's boatbuilding and construction industries.

Settlement.name: {=!}Thirthahalli
Settlement.text: {=!}Thirthahalli is a river-confluence village where the Tunga and Bhadra meet, sacred to the local temple tradition and prosperous from the arecanut cultivation of the surrounding Malnad hill country.

Settlement.name: {=!}Palladam
Settlement.text: {=!}Palladam is a hand-loom weaving village in the Coimbatore district where the cotton textile craft that feeds the Coromandel cloth trade has been practiced in every household for as long as memory holds.

Settlement.name: {=!}Pollachi
Settlement.text: {=!}Pollachi is a market town at the foot of the Palani Hills known for its coconut and banana cultivation and as the western gateway to the Anamalai forest range and its elephant country.

Settlement.name: {=!}Tiruppur
Settlement.text: {=!}Tiruppur is a weavers' colony in the Coimbatore district where the looms never stop running in any season, the cotton hosiery produced here reaching the markets of the entire Tamil south.

Settlement.name: {=!}Puttur
Settlement.text: {=!}Puttur is a trading village in the coastal Tulunadu country where arecanut and coconut from the Karnataka plains are exchanged for the rice and fish of the Malabar coast.

Settlement.name: {=!}Bantwal
Settlement.text: {=!}Bantwal is a river port on the Netravati where rice barges from the coastal plains discharge their cargo and boats loading pepper and cardamom from the Ghats ready themselves for the coastal voyage.

Settlement.name: {=!}Sullia
Settlement.text: {=!}Sullia is a frontier village at the western Ghat edge where the Kodava tribesmen of Coorg and Tamil traders from the plains meet in the weekly market under the shade of immense old-growth forest.

Settlement.name: {=!}Hoskote
Settlement.text: {=!}Hoskote is a cattle and silk market east of Bangalore where the Deccan trade converges with the Karnataka plateau routes, its weekly fair drawing buyers from a hundred miles in every direction.

Settlement.name: {=!}Yelahanka
Settlement.text: {=!}Yelahanka is a weavers' and farmers' village north of Bangalore where the fertile plateau soil grows ragi and mulberry in equal measure, the agricultural and textile economies intertwined.

Settlement.name: {=!}Doddaballapur
Settlement.text: {=!}Doddaballapur is a silk-weaving town north of Bangalore where the raw silk thread from the local mulberry farms is woven on hand-looms into fabric for the famous Mysore silk trade.

Settlement.name: {=!}Anekal
Settlement.text: {=!}Anekal is a frontier village on the uncertain Mysore-Hyderabad boundary where the zamindars skillfully play both powers against each other to maintain their independence.

Settlement.name: {=!}Nanjangud
Settlement.text: {=!}Nanjangud is a temple town on the Kabini River where the Srikanteshwara temple draws year-round pilgrims and the weekly market serves the farming villages of the southern Mysore plateau.

Settlement.name: {=!}Gundlupet
Settlement.text: {=!}Gundlupet is a forest-edge village at the gateway to the Nagarhole forests where elephant sightings are common on the road and the timber trade provides a modest living to the village's woodcutters.

Settlement.name: {=!}Hunsur
Settlement.text: {=!}Hunsur is a market town in the aromatic coffee-growing country of Coorg where the beans from the hill plantations are brought to the plains for onward sale to the coastal traders.

Settlement.name: {=!}Tiptur
Settlement.text: {=!}Tiptur is a coconut-processing village in the Tumkur district known for its copra trade, the dried coconut meat supplying the oil-presses and soap-makers of the entire Karnataka market.

Settlement.name: {=!}Tumkur
Settlement.text: {=!}Tumkur is a prosperous agricultural town north of Bangalore known for its silk, coconut, and groundnut cultivation and as the hub of northern Karnataka's trade routes to the Deccan.

Settlement.name: {=!}Kolar
Settlement.text: {=!}Kolar is a gold-mining town in the Karnataka plateau where ancient gold workings have been exploited since Vijayanagara times, the dust from the mines coating everything in a faint yellow.

Settlement.name: {=!}Channarayapatna
Settlement.text: {=!}Channarayapatna is a silk and cotton market town in the Hassan district on the road between Bangalore and Mysore.

Settlement.name: {=!}Chintamani
Settlement.text: {=!}Chintamani is a market town in the Chickballapur district on the road north of Bangalore where the weekly market serves the farming and weaving communities.

Settlement.name: {=!}Gauribidanur
Settlement.text: {=!}Gauribidanur is an agricultural town on the Pennar River in the Chickballapur district known for its groundnut cultivation and the weekly markets of the northern Karnataka plateau.

Settlement.name: {=!}Kunigal
Settlement.text: {=!}Kunigal is a silk-rearing village in the Tumkur district where mulberry cultivation and sericulture form the basis of the local economy.

Settlement.name: {=!}Magadi
Settlement.text: {=!}Magadi is a fortified village in the Ramanagara district known for its old fort and its position on the road between Bangalore and the Mysore kingdom's western territories.

Settlement.name: {=!}Kortagere
Settlement.text: {=!}Kortagere is a cattle market village on the Tumkur plateau where the hardy Hallikar bullocks prized by the Karnataka farmers are bred and traded.

Settlement.name: {=!}Pavagada
Settlement.text: {=!}Pavagada is a fortress village at the meeting point of the Andhra and Karnataka plateaus where the Mysore and Hyderabad boundaries remain perpetually disputed.

Settlement.name: {=!}Kodaikanal
Settlement.text: {=!}Kodaikanal is a cool hill station in the Palani Hills where merchants from the Coimbatore plains maintain summer homes and the medicinal herbs of the shola forests are collected.

Settlement.name: {=!}Palani
Settlement.text: {=!}Palani is a sacred hill town in the Palani Hills famous for the Murugan temple on its summit, drawing pilgrims from all over the Tamil country who climb on bare feet as an act of devotion.

Settlement.name: {=!}Shoranur
Settlement.text: {=!}Shoranur is a river-crossing village on the Bharathapuzha where the boats ferry goods and passengers between the Malabar coast and the Tamil country through the Palakkad gap.

Settlement.name: {=!}Ottapalam
Settlement.text: {=!}Ottapalam is a rice-farming and coconut-growing village in the fertile Malabar interior, supplying the Palakkad Fort garrison with provisions.

Settlement.name: {=!}Hosur
Settlement.text: {=!}Hosur is a frontier village at the Tamil-Karnataka border where the trade between the two linguistic regions passes through the market that straddles the cultural boundary.

Settlement.name: {=!}Denkanikottai
Settlement.text: {=!}Denkanikottai is a small market village at the edge of the Nilgiri forest range where the produce of the hills and the plains are exchanged.

Settlement.name: {=!}Hospet
Settlement.text: {=!}Hospet is the market town that grew beside the ruined capital of Vijayanagara, its weekly fair held in the shadow of the greatest medieval city in India.

Settlement.name: {=!}Sandur
Settlement.text: {=!}Sandur is an iron-ore mining village in the Bellary hills where the rich iron deposits have supported smelting since the Vijayanagara empire.

Settlement.name: {=!}Madanapalle
Settlement.text: {=!}Madanapalle is a market town in the Chittor district where the Telugu farmers and Telugu weavers exchange their produce in the weekly market of the Andhra borderlands.

Settlement.name: {=!}Punganur
Settlement.text: {=!}Punganur is a village in the Chittor district famous for its Punganur cow, the smallest cattle breed in the world, whose rich A2 milk is prized across the southern region.

Settlement.name: {=!}Beawar
Settlement.text: {=!}Beawar is a textile market town at the Aravalli foothills where cotton cloth from the surrounding Marwar villages is processed and sold to the merchants of Ajmer and Jodhpur.

Settlement.name: {=!}Nasirabad
Settlement.text: {=!}Nasirabad grew up around a Mughal military outpost on the Ajmer road, its cantonment still housing the troops that maintain the imperial presence in the heart of Rajputana.

Settlement.name: {=!}Nathdwara
Settlement.text: {=!}Nathdwara is the home of the Shrinathji temple where the black-stone image of Krishna was brought from Mathura fleeing Aurangzeb's iconoclasts, becoming the most important Vaishnava pilgrimage site in western India.

Settlement.name: {=!}Rajsamand
Settlement.text: {=!}Rajsamand takes its name from the great artificial lake built by Maharana Raj Singh, its marble embankments inscribed with what is said to be the longest Rajput inscription in the world.

Settlement.name: {=!}Pratapgarh
Settlement.text: {=!}Pratapgarh is a market town in the Chittor hinterland renowned for its gold and silver craftsmen who practice the distinctive thewa art of embedding gold designs in colored glass.

Settlement.name: {=!}Chomu
Settlement.text: {=!}Chomu is a desert-edge market north of Jaipur where camel caravans from Marwar and Shekhawati bring cotton, salt, and dried goods for the Jaipur merchants.

Settlement.name: {=!}Phulera
Settlement.text: {=!}Phulpur is an agricultural market town on the Jaipur plain where the seasonal harvests of mustard, bajra, and gram are assembled for the capital's grain merchants.

Settlement.name: {=!}Dudu
Settlement.text: {=!}Dudu is a desert village whose Rabari herding community drives flocks of Marwari sheep on the seasonal migration, famous locally for the quality of its hand-spun desert wool.

Settlement.name: {=!}Dabra
Settlement.text: {=!}Dabra is a limestone plateau village whose stone quarries supply the construction of the great Gwalior fort and the temples of the surrounding Vindhya hill country.

Settlement.name: {=!}Shivpuri
Settlement.text: {=!}Shivpuri is a forest village at the Vindhya edge where tigers still hunt in the teak groves and the Scindia chiefs occasionally venture for sport when they tire of court intrigues.

Settlement.name: {=!}Guna
Settlement.text: {=!}Guna is a cotton market on the Betwa watershed where the agricultural produce of the Malwa plateau is assembled for the merchants of Gwalior and the great trading city of Ujjain.

Settlement.name: {=!}Pokaran
Settlement.text: {=!}Pokaran is a desert caravanserai town on the western Rajputana trade route where salt, dried dates, and desert wool change hands between the Thar nomads and the Rajput merchants.

Settlement.name: {=!}Ramgarh
Settlement.text: {=!}Ramgarh is a sand-dune village where the Bishnoi community maintains sacred groves of khejri trees and protects the desert wildlife with a devotion that has led to martyrdom in the past.

Settlement.name: {=!}Bap
Settlement.text: {=!}Bap is a remote desert village connected to the outside world only by camel tracks, its population scraping a living from sparse rainfall and the salt pans of the neighbouring Rann.

Settlement.name: {=!}Phalodi
Settlement.text: {=!}Phalodi is a salt-trading town at the Thar edge where the dry lake beds yield a seasonal harvest of crystalline salt that is the economic foundation of the entire desert hinterland.

Settlement.name: {=!}Nainwa
Settlement.text: {=!}Nainwa is a marble-quarry village in the Bundi hills where the white stone that decorates the palaces of Bundi and Kota is hewn from the Vindhya hillside.

Settlement.name: {=!}Hindoli
Settlement.text: {=!}Hindoli is a forest village in the Bundi hinterland where the Bhil and Meena tribal communities maintain their ancient way of life at the margins of Rajput territory.

Settlement.name: {=!}Nokha
Settlement.text: {=!}Nokha is a market town in Bikaner's herdsmen country where camels, desert wool, and dried goods are traded at the weekly fair attended by the desert communities from miles around.

Settlement.name: {=!}Kolayat
Settlement.text: {=!}Kolayat is a sacred lake town in the Bikaner desert where the sage Kapil Muni is said to have meditated, its November cattle fair drawing half a million pilgrims to bathe in the holy water.

Settlement.name: {=!}Deshnok
Settlement.text: {=!}Deshnok is the home of the Karni Mata temple where rats are held sacred and fed by the priests, pilgrims coming from across Rajputana to seek the goddess's blessing before long desert journeys.

Settlement.name: {=!}Pali
Settlement.text: {=!}Pali is a textile market on the Bandi River where the tie-dye fabric called bandhani is produced in brilliant colors by the Khatri community and sold across western India.

Settlement.name: {=!}Sojat
Settlement.text: {=!}Sojat is a village of rose gardens whose attar is distilled for the perfumers of Jodhpur and Jaipur, the scent of Damask rose filling the lanes in the brief but intense flowering season.

Settlement.name: {=!}Begun
Settlement.text: {=!}Begun is a village in the Chittorgarh district where the ancient craft of block-printing fabric in natural dyes has been practiced for generations by the Chhipa community.

Settlement.name: {=!}Rashmi
Settlement.text: {=!}Rashmi is a farming village in the Chittorgarh hinterland whose fields of wheat and mustard supply the fort garrison and the pilgrims visiting the battlefield temples.

Settlement.name: {=!}Khamnor
Settlement.text: {=!}Khamnor is a village in the Rajsamand district famous for its marble quarries that supply the white stone used in the temples and palaces of the Mewar kingdom.

Settlement.name: {=!}Saira
Settlement.text: {=!}Saira is a farming village in the Udaipur hills whose terraced fields and cattle grazing support the rural communities beneath the walls of Kumbhalgarh.

Settlement.name: {=!}Karauli
Settlement.text: {=!}Karauli is the fortified seat of the Jadaun Rajput clan where the Karauli maharaja maintains an ancient court tradition and the Krishna temple draws pilgrims from the Braj region.

Settlement.name: {=!}Gangapur
Settlement.text: {=!}Gangapur is a market town in the Sawai Madhopur district where the agricultural produce of the eastern Rajputana plains is assembled for the Jaipur merchants.

Settlement.name: {=!}Balesar
Settlement.text: {=!}Balesar is a small fortified village in the Jodhpur district where the Rathore clan maintains a subordinate chief whose loyalty to the Jodhpur throne is periodically tested.

Settlement.name: {=!}Shergarh
Settlement.text: {=!}Shergarh is a desert village in the Jodhpur hinterland whose population of camel herders and farmers supports the garrison of the great Mehrangarh above the city.

Settlement.name: {=!}Bajju
Settlement.text: {=!}Bajju is a remote desert village in the Bikaner district connected to the outside world by camel track, its salt pans the economic foundation of the community.

Settlement.name: {=!}Lunkaransar
Settlement.text: {=!}Lunkaransar is a salt lake village in the Bikaner desert where the seasonal crystallization of the saline lake provides the primary commodity for trade.

Settlement.name: {=!}Jhalawar
Settlement.text: {=!}Jhalawar is the fortified seat of the Jhala clan whose chiefs occupy a strategic position in the southeastern Rajputana between the Rajput highlands and the Malwa plateau.

Settlement.name: {=!}Manoharthana
Settlement.text: {=!}Manoharthana is a village in the Jhalawar district where the rocky Hadoti plateau gives way to the Malwa plains and the farming communities begin to shift from millet to wheat.

Settlement.name: {=!}Kekri
Settlement.text: {=!}Kekri is a market town in the Ajmer district where the weekly market serves the agricultural communities of the Taragarh foothills.

Settlement.name: {=!}Kishangarh
Settlement.text: {=!}Kishangarh is famous throughout the Mughal world as the home of the Kishangarh school of miniature painting, whose idealized depictions of Krishna and Radha are collected by connoisseurs.

Settlement.name: {=!}Merta
Settlement.text: {=!}Merta is the birthplace of Mirabai, the poet-saint who renounced her Rajput marriage to become a wandering devotee of Krishna, her bhajans sung across northern India.

Settlement.name: {=!}Mundwa
Settlement.text: {=!}Mundwa is a desert market village in the Nagaur district where the seasonal cattle fairs draw drovers and buyers from across the western Rajputana.

Settlement.name: {=!}Shirur
Settlement.text: {=!}Shirur is a market village on the Bhima tributary where the wheat and jowar of the northern Pune district is assembled for the capital markets of the Maratha confederacy.

Settlement.name: {=!}Baramati
Settlement.text: {=!}Baramati is a prosperous agricultural town in the Bhima valley where Brahmin landowners have introduced grape cultivation, the table fruit finding ready buyers in the Pune markets.

Settlement.name: {=!}Daund
Settlement.text: {=!}Daund is a river-junction village where the Bhima and Man rivers meet, important as a crossing point for the armies and merchants moving between the Deccan plateau and the eastern Marathwada.

Settlement.name: {=!}Indapur
Settlement.text: {=!}Indapur is a large agricultural village in the Bhima valley where cotton cultivation and the weekly market serve the prosperous farming communities of the northern Pune district.

Settlement.name: {=!}Sangli
Settlement.text: {=!}Sangli is the turmeric capital of the Deccan, the golden spice grown in the rich Krishna valley soil here considered the finest in the subcontinent and traded from Bombay to Bengal.

Settlement.name: {=!}Miraj
Settlement.text: {=!}Miraj is a town renowned for classical music, its hereditary craftsmen producing the finest sitars and tanpuras in India, supplying court musicians from Delhi to Tanjore.

Settlement.name: {=!}Tasgaon
Settlement.text: {=!}Tasgaon is a sugarcane village in the Krishna valley where the crushing mills run through winter and the jaggery stacked in golden blocks sustains the sweet tooth of the Maratha armies.

Settlement.name: {=!}Navsari
Settlement.text: {=!}Navsari is the principal Parsi settlement on the Gujarat coast where the ancient fire-worshipping refugees from Persia maintain their sacred flame and their prosperous trading tradition.

Settlement.name: {=!}Bardoli
Settlement.text: {=!}Bardoli is a sugarcane and mango village in the Surat hinterland where the famous Alphonso mango is grown and the fine Surat cotton is assembled before the journey to the port.

Settlement.name: {=!}Mandvi
Settlement.text: {=!}Mandvi is a small port on the Tapi estuary where country boats load cotton, sesame, and timber for the coastal trade with Bombay and Surat.

Settlement.name: {=!}Karad
Settlement.text: {=!}Karad is a market town at the confluence of the Krishna and Koyna rivers where the grain and cotton of the western Deccan meet the roads running down to the Konkan ports.

Settlement.name: {=!}Wai
Settlement.text: {=!}Wai is a temple town on the Krishna where the ghats are perpetually crowded with pilgrims and the famous Ganpati temple draws devotees from all over Maharashtra to its elaborate festivals.

Settlement.name: {=!}Phaltan
Settlement.text: {=!}Phaltan is a jagir village whose Nimbalkar lords hold the land in hereditary service to the Maratha confederacy, the sugarcane fields and cotton orchards supporting a comfortable estate.

Settlement.name: {=!}Mhasvad
Settlement.text: {=!}Mhasvad is a cattle-market village in the drought-prone Marathwada where the hardy Maratha bullocks are bred and sold to the farming communities of the plateau.

Settlement.name: {=!}Igatpuri
Settlement.text: {=!}Igatpuri sits at the crest of the Thal Ghat pass where the Marathas collect customs on every bale of goods crossing between the Deccan and the Konkan, one of the confederacy's most profitable posts.

Settlement.name: {=!}Sinnar
Settlement.text: {=!}Sinnar is an ancient village on the Godavari tributary where Yadava-era temples and Mughal-period mosques stand in surprising proximity, the population divided between Hindu farmers and Muslim weavers.

Settlement.name: {=!}Niphad
Settlement.text: {=!}Niphad is a grape-growing village on the upper Godavari where the dryland viticulture produces table grapes sold in the Nashik markets and to the wine-drinking households of the Pune nobility.

Settlement.name: {=!}Mahad
Settlement.text: {=!}Mahad is a sacred Konkan town on the Savitri River where the Ganesh temple and the river confluence draw pilgrims from the coastal communities.

Settlement.name: {=!}Poladpur
Settlement.text: {=!}Poladpur is a village at the foot of the Amba Ghat where the coastal road from the Konkan begins the climb to the Deccan plateau.

Settlement.name: {=!}Mahabaleshwar
Settlement.text: {=!}Mahabaleshwar is a cool hill retreat on the highest point of the Sahyadri range where the Maratha chiefs maintain summer camps away from the monsoon heat of the Deccan.

Settlement.name: {=!}Panchgani
Settlement.text: {=!}Panchgani is a plateau village above the Krishna valley where five hills converge and the strawberry cultivation introduced recently has found a ready market among Pune's wealthy.

Settlement.name: {=!}Rajgad
Settlement.text: {=!}Rajgad is a village at the foot of the ancient Maratha fort where Shivaji kept his throne before Raigadh, its forest approaches making it one of the most difficult fortresses to besiege.

Settlement.name: {=!}Bhor
Settlement.text: {=!}Bhor is a market village in the Sahyadri passes that grew around the Bhor ghat customs post where Maratha toll-collectors extract revenue from the Konkan-Deccan trade.

Settlement.name: {=!}Ajara
Settlement.text: {=!}Ajara is a hilly Kolhapur district village where the jungle communities maintain the old Maratha woodland tradition of hit-and-run resistance against any occupying army.

Settlement.name: {=!}Radhanagari
Settlement.text: {=!}Radhanagari is a forest village in the Kolhapur hills known for the ancient Deshkari cattle breed and the wildlife sanctuary that surrounds it.

Settlement.name: {=!}Junnar
Settlement.text: {=!}Junnar is an ancient Buddhist cave-town north of Pune where the Naneghat pass and the surrounding hills preserve some of the finest rock-cut Buddhist architecture in western India.

Settlement.name: {=!}Alephata
Settlement.text: {=!}Alephata is a market village on the Pravara River in the northern Pune district whose weekly market serves the agricultural communities of the upper Godavari.

Settlement.name: {=!}Khandala
Settlement.text: {=!}Khandala is a Ghat village at the crest of the Bhor pass where the road from Bombay begins the descent to the Konkan, famous for its views over the coastal plain.

Settlement.name: {=!}Ambavane
Settlement.text: {=!}Ambavane is a small village in the Sahyadri where the mango orchards of the Konkan slopes produce the famous Ambavane Alphonso that commands the highest price in the Pune market.

Settlement.name: {=!}Palghar
Settlement.text: {=!}Palghar is a coastal market on the northern Konkan where fishing communities, coconut farmers, and the inland trade routes converge in the weekly market.

Settlement.name: {=!}Dahanu
Settlement.text: {=!}Dahanu is a Konkan coastal village famous for its sapota and Alphonso mango orchards whose fruit is traded to the Bombay and Surat markets.

Settlement.name: {=!}Akkalkot
Settlement.text: {=!}Akkalkot is a sacred Lingayat and Dattatreya pilgrimage village in the Solapur district where the Maharaj of Akkalkot is revered as a living saint by his devotees.

Settlement.name: {=!}Barsi
Settlement.text: {=!}Barsi is a market town on the upper Sina River in the Solapur district where the weekly market connects the farming villages of the eastern Deccan plateau.

Settlement.name: {=!}Morinda
Settlement.text: {=!}Morinda is a Sikh trading village on the Grand Trunk Road between Ambala and Ludhiana where the produce of the Malwa countryside is exchanged in the weekly market.

Settlement.name: {=!}Fatehgarh
Settlement.text: {=!}Fatehgarh is a Sikh garrison on the Sutlej River where the Khalsa maintains a crossing-point force watching the river boundary between the Malwa and Majha regions of the Punjab.

Settlement.name: {=!}Bassi
Settlement.text: {=!}Bassi is an agricultural village in the Rupnagar foothills where the wheat and sugarcane harvests feed both the Anandpur pilgrims and the armies of the Khalsa.

Settlement.name: {=!}Phagwara
Settlement.text: {=!}Phagwara is a market town on the Grand Trunk Road between Ambala and Lahore whose highway position supports an unusually prosperous local trading economy.

Settlement.name: {=!}Nakodar
Settlement.text: {=!}Nakodar is a cotton textile market in the Jalandhar Doab where the Punjab plain's cotton meets the Himalayan foothills trade in wool and timber.

Settlement.name: {=!}Sheikhupura
Settlement.text: {=!}Sheikhupura preserves Mughal Jahangir's Hiran Minar deer tower in the fields outside the city, the hunting park now farmed but the minaret still standing as a monument to imperial leisure.

Settlement.name: {=!}Gujranwala
Settlement.text: {=!}Gujranwala is a prosperous farming town on the Grand Trunk Road known for wheat cultivation and the potters who produce distinctive earthenware sold throughout the upper Punjab.

Settlement.name: {=!}Wazirabad
Settlement.text: {=!}Wazirabad is a river-crossing town on the Chenab where armies and merchants have forded since antiquity, its ferrymen among the most experienced river navigators in the Punjab.

Settlement.name: {=!}Khanewal
Settlement.text: {=!}Khanewal is a cotton and wheat market on the Multan-Lahore road where the agricultural produce of the Rechna Doab is assembled for the great trading cities of the Punjab.

Settlement.name: {=!}Lodhran
Settlement.text: {=!}Lodhran is a cotton village in the Bahawalpur borderlands where the Sikh writ barely reaches and the local tribesmen administer their own justice according to ancient tribal custom.

Settlement.name: {=!}Muzaffargarh
Settlement.text: {=!}Muzaffargarh stands at the confluence of the Chenab and Jhelum rivers, its position making it a natural marketplace for the river trade of the entire western Punjab.

Settlement.name: {=!}Batala
Settlement.text: {=!}Batala is renowned for the quality of its copper and brassware, the village's artisans supplying cooking vessels, lamps, and hookah fittings to the households of the Punjab.

Settlement.name: {=!}Gurdaspur
Settlement.text: {=!}Gurdaspur is a border town where the Punjab plains meet the first Shivalik ridges, its garrison watching the hill passes through which raiders from the mountain chieftains periodically descend.

Settlement.name: {=!}Pathankot
Settlement.text: {=!}Pathankot is the gateway to the Kangra valley at the end of the Punjab plains, controlling the routes to Jammu and Kashmir and the mountain states of the Himalayan foothills.

Settlement.name: {=!}Gujrat
Settlement.text: {=!}Gujrat is a Punjab town famous for its skilled furniture-makers who work the sheesham wood of the Jhelum riverbanks into the carved charpoys and cabinets sought by households across the region.

Settlement.name: {=!}Daska
Settlement.text: {=!}Daska is a leather-working town on the Deg River where cobblers and tanners supply footwear to the soldiers and farmers of the upper Punjab.

Settlement.name: {=!}Pasrur
Settlement.text: {=!}Pasrur is a border village on the Sialkot road where Afghan raiders and Sikh border patrols maintain a constant low-level contest for control of the farmlands and their revenues.

Settlement.name: {=!}Ropar
Settlement.text: {=!}Ropar is the ancient mound town on the Sutlej River where Harappan civilization settlements have been discovered beneath the current village, its river crossing as ancient as civilization itself.

Settlement.name: {=!}Chamkaur
Settlement.text: {=!}Chamkaur is sacred to the Sikhs as the site of the last stand of Guru Gobind Singh's sons, who died fighting the Mughal army with such valor that the story is told in every Sikh household.

Settlement.name: {=!}Doraha
Settlement.text: {=!}Doraha is a river crossing town on the Sutlej where the major road from Delhi to Lahore crosses the river, its ferry and bridge making it a natural toll point.

Settlement.name: {=!}Khanna
Settlement.text: {=!}Khanna is a grain-market town in the Ludhiana district where the famous Punjab wheat is assembled in huge quantities for the merchants of the grand trunk road.

Settlement.name: {=!}Hazro
Settlement.text: {=!}Hazro is a market town on the Indus-Haro watershed between Attock and the Punjab plains, its bazaars supplying the crossing point communities of the upper Indus.

Settlement.name: {=!}Fatehjang
Settlement.text: {=!}Fatehjang is a small village in the Attock district whose name commemorates a military victory, its position on the road from Rawalpindi to Attock making it a minor garrison post.

Settlement.name: {=!}Abohar
Settlement.text: {=!}Abohar is a desert-edge market town in the Firozpur district where the Punjab plains meet the Thar Desert, its weekly market a meeting point for the agricultural and pastoral economies.

Settlement.name: {=!}Fazilka
Settlement.text: {=!}Fazilka is a border village in the Firozpur district on the edge of the Thar where the Punjab irrigation begins to fail and the desert economy of camel herding takes over.

Settlement.name: {=!}Sultanpur
Settlement.text: {=!}Sultanpur Lodhi on the Beas River is sacred as the town where Guru Nanak spent fourteen years as a government official before his spiritual transformation, the first place he composed his hymns.

Settlement.name: {=!}Hoshiarpur
Settlement.text: {=!}Hoshiarpur is the market center of the Sikh foothills belt where the Himalayan produce of timber, honey, and ginger meets the Punjab plains trade in cotton and grain.

Settlement.name: {=!}Chunian
Settlement.text: {=!}Chunian is a market town in the Punjab whose fields produce excellent wheat, the local varieties selected over generations for the productivity of the fertile Bari Doab soils.

Settlement.name: {=!}Pattoki
Settlement.text: {=!}Pattoki is an agricultural village in the Okara district known for its cotton and wheat cultivation, the produce supplying the Lahore markets through the weekly grain fairs.

Settlement.name: {=!}Shahkot
Settlement.text: {=!}Shahkot is a market village in the Nankana Sahib district whose weekly fair connects the Ravi-Beas Doab farming communities with the Lahore merchants.

Settlement.name: {=!}Zira
Settlement.text: {=!}Zira is a prosperous agricultural town in the Firozpur district known for its wheat cultivation and the basmati rice grown in the canal-irrigated fields.

Settlement.name: {=!}Landikotal
Settlement.text: {=!}Landikotal is the tribal market at the top of the Khyber Pass where the Afridi tribesmen sell their smuggled goods to merchants from both the Afghan and Indian sides.

Settlement.name: {=!}Bara
Settlement.text: {=!}Bara is a tribal market village just outside the Khyber Pass in the Peshawar plain where the border economy of the pass spills over into the agricultural world beyond.

Settlement.name: {=!}Chakwal
Settlement.text: {=!}Chakwal is a district market town on the Pothohar Plateau where the salt range begins and the distinctive pinkish Himalayan salt from the Khewra mines is traded.

Settlement.name: {=!}Khewra
Settlement.text: {=!}Khewra is the village above the world's second-largest salt mine, its pink salt crystal pillars buried in the mountain since before human memory, now worked by miners who are born and die in its tunnels.
